assertions.title: Struct codegen preserves shape and visibility

steps.action: Run focused Alcotest codegen coverage through the Nix CLI surface
steps.expected: The selected test group for VAL-CODEGEN-001 passes.
steps.observed: [OK]          struct                    0   basic struct. | [OK]          struct                    1   pub/private fields. | [OK]          struct-impl               0   constructor + methods. | [OK]          struct-impl               1   value receiver. | Test Successful in 1.244s. 4 tests run.

commands.command: nix develop --quiet -c dune exec ./test/test_codegen.exe -- test --color=never struct 0-1

commands.command: nix develop --quiet -c dune exec ./test/test_codegen.exe -- test --color=never array 0-4

assertions.title: Nested optional forms remain distinguishable

steps.action: Compile a dedicated CLI fixture to Go
steps.expected: rgoc succeeds and emits generated Go that preserves nested Option structure.
steps.observed: Compilation succeeded, but the emitted Go lowered `Option<Option<i64>>` to `**int64` and then generated a type switch over undefined `OptionSome`/`OptionNone` symbols.

steps.expected: go build succeeds and the runtime can distinguish outer None vs Some(None) vs Some(Some(9)).
steps.observed: go build failed with `v (variable of type *int64) is not an interface`, `undefined: OptionSome`, and `undefined: OptionNone` in the generated file.

assertions.issues: Nested `Option<Option<i64>>` compiled into invalid Go: the outer option became `**int64`, then the generated match attempted a type switch on `*int64` and referenced undefined `OptionSome`/`OptionNone` helpers.

summary: Validated struct/enum/option/array/keyword Phase 7 assertions using focused Alcotest runs plus direct CLI fixtures for Option representations.
